title: Identify A Faulted Tipper From The Operator Station Fault Beacon And Return It To Service
summary: Use the operator station fault beacon to identify a tipper fault, correct the fault, and return the tipper to service. The source supports the high-level troubleshooting sequence but does not provide the detailed fault-correction method in the cited troubleshooting entry.
when_to_use: Use when too many AGVs are being directed to the hospital and the troubleshooting guidance indicates a tipper fault should be identified by looking for a fault beacon on the operator station, then corrected and returned to service.
not_for: Do not use as a complete repair procedure when fault-specific correction steps are required but not provided in the source.
Do not use when the operator station fault beacon cannot be correlated to a specific tipper from the available source evidence.

responsibilities: Look for the fault beacon indicating the tipper fault.
Use applicable documented fault-specific procedure if available.
Return the tipper to service or escalate if unsupported by source detail.

safety_notes: The candidate marks this procedure as not support-safe.
The cited troubleshooting source does not provide detailed correction or recovery controls; use only documented fault-specific procedures when available.
Do not invent reset, override, or return-to-service actions not explicitly supported by the source.

system_preconditions: A tipper fault condition is suspected in the context of too many AGVs being directed to the hospital.
The operator station fault beacon is available for observation.

visual_references.description: Operator station HMI view that may help orient the user to operator-station fault recovery context.
Operator station HMI screen snippet associated with fault reset context on the operator station.
Photo of operator station controls used in a separate non-recoverable fault recovery context; useful only as operator-station orientation.
Operator station screen associated with a tipper-related AGV fault procedure; useful as related operator-station context.
Hospital station artifact relevant to downstream hospital handling context when faults divert AGVs.
Related fault recovery screen for AGV fault handling; not the primary evidence for this runbook.
Operator station HMI screen from tipper commissioning; useful only for operator-station orientation.
Maintenance diagram for a specific tipper component replacement; only relevant if a separate fault-specific procedure points to this repair.

steps.title: Check the operator station for the fault beacon
steps.purpose: Identify whether the troubleshooting condition points to a tipper fault visible from the operator station.
steps.instruction: Go to the operator station and look for the fault beacon that indicates the tipper fault.
steps.expected_result: A fault beacon is observed on the operator station, or no beacon is found.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station clearly indicates the fault condition by beacon status so the troubleshooting path can continue.
steps.failure_condition: No fault beacon is visible, or the beacon indication is unclear from the available source guidance.

actions.label: Inspect operator station fault indication
actions.exact_action: Look for fault beacon on the operator station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: General operator station HMI layout for orientation while locating fault-related indications.
Operator station screen context associated with fault handling and RESET-related controls; use only for orientation, not as a prescribed action.
Physical operator station controls for general station identification.

stop_or_escalate_if: The fault beacon is present but its meaning or associated tipper cannot be determined from the source.
No fault beacon is visible and the troubleshooting symptom persists.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Too many AGVs being directed to the hospital... Tipper fault... Look for fault beacon on the operator station.
The troubleshooting section states that a tipper fault associated with too many AGVs being directed to the hospital can be identified by looking for a fault beacon on the operator station.

steps.title: Identify the affected tipper
steps.purpose: Determine which tipper is faulted so the correct corrective procedure can be applied.
steps.instruction: Identify the tipper associated with the fault beacon.
steps.healthy_condition: A specific tipper can be associated with the observed fault indication.
steps.failure_condition: The source does not provide enough detail to map the beacon to a specific tipper.

actions.label: Determine affected tipper
actions.exact_action: Identify the tipper associated with the fault beacon.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Related operator station screen context for selecting or confirming an affected tipper in a separate tipper fault scenario.
Operator station HMI context showing tipper-related station interface orientation.

stop_or_escalate_if: The affected tipper cannot be identified from the beacon and available source material.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Too many AGVs being directed to the hospital... Tipper fault... Look for fault beacon on the operator station. Correct the fault. Return the tipper to service.

steps.title: Correct the fault using applicable documented procedure
steps.purpose: Resolve the identified tipper fault using source-supported documentation rather than undocumented actions.
steps.instruction: Correct the fault using the applicable documented fault-specific procedure if available.
steps.expected_result: The fault condition is corrected according to a documented procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: A fault-specific documented procedure is available and can be followed for the identified tipper fault.
steps.failure_condition: The troubleshooting source identifies the need to correct the fault but does not provide the correction steps, or the available documentation does not resolve the issue.

actions.label: Locate fault-specific procedure
actions.exact_action: Correct the fault using the applicable documented fault-specific procedure if available.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Only if a separate documented fault-specific procedure identifies flat-flex cable replacement as the required correction.
Related fault recovery context only if the identified issue involves AGV fault handling documented elsewhere in the same source.

stop_or_escalate_if: The fault beacon is present but the source does not provide the fault-specific correction steps.
The identified fault requires a repair or recovery procedure not included in the supplied evidence.
Any required corrective action would require unsupported commands, settings changes, or maintenance actions not explicitly documented in the packet.

source_evidence.note: Candidate summary states the troubleshooting table does not provide the detailed correction steps.

steps.title: Return the tipper to service
steps.purpose: Restore the corrected tipper to operational service after the fault has been addressed.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is back in service after fault correction.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper is available for normal operation and no longer remains in the faulted state.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper cannot be returned to service after the fault is corrected, or the source does not provide the exact return-to-service method.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station controls in related return-to-automatic-operation context; use only if a separate documented fault-specific procedure directs these controls.
Hospital station context if AGV/tote flow normalization is part of the broader recovery outcome.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tipper cannot be returned to service after the fault is corrected.
The source does not provide the exact return-to-service actions needed for the identified fault.

success_criteria: The faulted tipper is identified from the operator station fault beacon.
The applicable fault is corrected using documented source-supported guidance if available.
The tipper is returned to service.
healthy_conditions: A fault beacon on the operator station can be used to identify the tipper fault condition.
The affected tipper can be identified.
The tipper no longer remains faulted and is returned to service.
failure_conditions: The fault beacon is present but the source does not provide fault-specific correction steps.
The affected tipper cannot be identified from the available source evidence.
The tipper cannot be returned to service after correction.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the fault beacon is present but the source does not provide the fault-specific correction steps.
Escalate if the tipper cannot be returned to service after the fault is corrected.
Escalate if the operator station indication cannot be mapped to a specific tipper using the supplied source evidence.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station physical controls for orientation.
Related tipper fault operator station screen context.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Too many AGVs being directed to the hospital... Tipper fault... Look for fault beacon on the operator station. Correct the fault. Return the tipper to service.

missing_details: The source does not describe the appearance or exact behavior of the operator station fault beacon.
The source does not explain how to map the fault beacon to a specific tipper.
The source does not provide the fault-specific correction steps.
The source does not provide the exact return-to-service actions.
